evals.prompt:
I have 4 research findings from a study on enterprise AI adoption. Please produce an executive summary using the exec-summary genre template and write it to exec-summary.md. Here are the findings:

---
Finding 1 (f_market_1): Enterprise AI adoption reached 72% of Fortune 500 firms in 2024, up from 41% in 2022. Source: https://gartner.com/reports/ai-enterprise-2024. Dimension: market. Verdict: survived.

Finding 2 (f_market_2): Implementation costs average $2.3M for mid-market deployments, with a 14-month median payback period. Source: https://mckinsey.com/ai-roi-2024. Dimension: market. Verdict: survived.

Finding 3 (f_technical_1): LLM-based automation reduces analyst hours by 40% in back-office workflows, per 3 controlled pilots. Source: https://hbr.org/llm-pilots-2024. Dimension: technical. Verdict: survived.

Finding 4 (f_technical_2): Data governance gaps blocked deployment in 31% of attempted rollouts. Source: https://forrester.com/ai-blockers-2024. Dimension: technical. Verdict: survived.

The decision being supported: whether to approve a $5M AI transformation budget for Q1 2025.
evals.expected_output: An executive summary written to exec-summary.md containing all four required sections (BLUF, Key Findings, Recommendation, Risks & Caveats), front matter, a numbered footnote list, at least one quantified figure, and no internal MIF plumbing (no urn:mif: URNs, no f_market_1-style internal IDs in the body).

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Document opens with a BLUF section
Document contains a Key Findings section
Document contains a Recommendation section
Document contains a Risks & Caveats section
At least one quantified figure from the findings is carried through
Internal MIF URNs do not appear in the output
Internal finding ID handles do not leak into the output body
No appendices — the genre prohibits them
No bibliography — genre uses footnotes only

expectations: The BLUF section appears first, before any context or background, and states the conclusion and recommended action in one paragraph.
The Key Findings section contains 3-5 bullets, each with a single fact and its business consequence, and at least some bullets include inline citation markers like [1] or [2].
The Recommendation section contains one specific, actionable directive that addresses the $5M budget decision with What, Why, How, and Risk elements.
The footnote list at the end resolves inline citation markers to primary-source URLs (the gartner.com, mckinsey.com, hbr.org, or forrester.com sources), not to internal finding IDs.
The document is written in active voice throughout, with no passive constructions like 'it was found that' or 'analysis showed'.

evals.prompt:
Produce an executive summary using the exec-summary genre and write it to exec-summary.md. I need a brief for the board on entering the Southeast Asian logistics market. Here are the findings — note that one was falsified and must be excluded:

---
Finding A (f_landscape_1): Southeast Asia logistics market is valued at $51B and growing at 9% CAGR through 2028. Source: https://statista.com/sea-logistics-2024. Dimension: landscape. Verdict: survived.

Finding B (f_landscape_2): Three incumbents (DHL, Ninja Van, J&T) control 58% of last-mile volume. Source: https://pitchbook.com/sea-logistics-players. Dimension: landscape. Verdict: survived.

Finding C (f_landscape_3): Regulatory clearance timeline averages 18 months across 6 ASEAN markets. Source: https://aseansec.org/logistics-regs. Dimension: landscape. Verdict: survived.

Finding D (f_landscape_4): Market entry cost estimated at $4.2M based on 2019 data from a single private source with no corroboration. Source: https://internal-memo.example.com/2019. Dimension: landscape. Verdict: falsified.

The decision being supported: whether to greenlight a market entry feasibility study in Q2 2025.
evals.expected_output: An executive summary that excludes the falsified finding (Finding D / $4.2M cost estimate) entirely, covers the other three findings, and produces all required sections with front matter and footnotes.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
The falsified finding's unique figure does not appear anywhere in the output
The falsified finding's source URL is not cited
A surviving quantified finding is present in the output
BLUF section is present
Recommendation section is present
Internal MIF URNs do not leak into output
The falsified finding's internal handle does not appear

expectations: The falsified finding (Finding D, $4.2M cost estimate) is completely absent from the output — it is not mentioned, alluded to, or hedged against.
The Recommendation addresses the feasibility study decision with a clear directive, not a generic 'consider further research' non-answer.
The Risks & Caveats section acknowledges the limited findings set (three surviving facts) and the absence of cost-estimate data as a decision risk.
Footnotes resolve only to the three surviving source URLs (statista.com, pitchbook.com, aseansec.org).

evals.prompt:
Use the exec-summary genre template to create an executive summary and write it to exec-summary.md. Context: I'm advising a hospital network on adopting AI-assisted diagnostics. One finding is inconclusive, which should be disclosed in Risks & Caveats. Another is weakened — also needs a confidence hedge there.

Findings:

---
Finding 1 (f_clinical_1): AI diagnostic tools reduced radiologist review time by 35% across 12 published trials in 2023-2024. Source: https://nejm.org/ai-radiology-2024. Dimension: clinical. Verdict: survived.

Finding 2 (f_compliance_1): FDA cleared 15 new AI diagnostic devices in 2024, signaling regulatory acceptance. Source: https://fda.gov/ai-devices-2024. Dimension: regulatory. Verdict: weakened. Verdict basis: Clearances cited cover narrow device classes only; generalizability is limited.

Finding 3 (f_clinical_2): Hospital AI programs show 22% reduction in diagnostic error rates when combined with clinician review protocols. Source: https://lancet.com/ai-hospitals-2024. Dimension: clinical. Verdict: survived.

Finding 4 (f_compliance_2): Long-term liability framework for AI diagnostic errors remains unresolved at the federal level. Source: https://healthaffairs.org/ai-liability-2024. Dimension: regulatory. Verdict: inconclusive. Verdict basis: Proposed rules exist but no final rulemaking.

Decision supported: whether to proceed with a pilot deployment of AI diagnostics in two radiology departments.
evals.expected_output: An executive summary that includes a Risks & Caveats section explicitly flagging the two uncertain findings (weakened and inconclusive verdicts) with confidence hedges, while still using all four findings in Key Findings. The document must have all required sections, front matter, no internal MIF leakage, and footnotes.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
BLUF section is present
Key Findings section is present
Recommendation section is present
Risks & Caveats section is present
At least one quantified figure is carried through
Internal MIF URNs do not appear
Internal finding handles do not leak into body
No appendices — genre prohibits them

expectations: The Risks & Caveats section explicitly notes that Finding 2 (FDA clearances) is limited in generalizability, using hedging language such as 'limited', 'uncertain', 'estimated', or equivalent.
The Risks & Caveats section explicitly notes that the liability framework (Finding 4) is unresolved or inconclusive, and does not present it as established fact.
The BLUF paragraph recommends a clear action regarding the two-department pilot without hedging into an extended methods discussion.
The document is 1-2 pages in length — not a multi-section research report with method sections, alternatives analysis, or appendices.

evals.prompt:
Produce a PTES-style penetration-test report leadership summary by composing the exec-summary genre into PTES mode. Write it to ptes-report.md. Render the executive/leadership summary expanded with the PTES sub-elements Posture, Risk Profile, and Roadmap, in addition to BLUF and Key Findings. Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_pt_1 | title: External attack surface exposed an unauthenticated admin panel | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/admin-panel
- id: urn:mif:f_pt_2 | title: Password policy permits credential stuffing at scale | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/cred-stuffing
- id: urn:mif:f_pt_3 | title: Network segmentation limits lateral movement from the DMZ | dimension: security | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/segmentation
evals.expected_output: A PTES leadership summary with BLUF and Key Findings PLUS the PTES sub-elements Posture, Risk Profile, and Roadmap. No urn:mif: leakage.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file created
BLUF still present in PTES composable mode (additive)
PTES leadership sub-element Posture rendered
PTES leadership sub-element Risk Profile rendered
PTES leadership sub-element Roadmap rendered
No raw urn:mif: identifiers leak
No internal MIF finding IDs leak

expectations: The PTES leadership summary contains a security Posture statement, a business Risk Profile derived from the findings, and a prioritized remediation Roadmap, each distinct and composed on top of the standard BLUF/Key Findings rather than replacing them.
The output does not claim strict ESOMAR-format conformance (ESOMAR is an ethics code); the PTES Posture/Risk Profile/Roadmap sub-elements appear only because PTES mode was requested.

evals.prompt:
Produce an ESOMAR-style market-research management summary by composing the exec-summary genre into ESOMAR mode. Write it to esomar-report.md. Render the exec-summary as the management summary that introduces a fuller market-research study (the leadership-summary section only, sized for embedding, not the whole study). Carry the ESOMAR ethics-code-not-format caveat explicitly. Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_market_1 | title: SEA logistics market valued at $51B, growing 9% CAGR through 2028 | dimension: market | verdict: survived | citation: https://statista.com/sea-logistics-2024
- id: urn:mif:f_market_2 | title: Three incumbents control 58% of last-mile volume | dimension: market | verdict: survived | citation: https://pitchbook.com/sea-logistics-players
- id: urn:mif:f_market_3 | title: Regulatory clearance averages 18 months across 6 ASEAN markets | dimension: market | verdict: survived | citation: https://aseansec.org/logistics-regs
evals.expected_output: An ESOMAR-style management summary (BLUF, Key Findings, Recommendation, Risks & Caveats) that explicitly carries the caveat that ESOMAR is an ethics code and not a report format, and that does NOT claim ESOMAR-format conformance. No urn:mif: leakage.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file created
BLUF still present in ESOMAR composable mode (additive)
ESOMAR ethics-code-not-format caveat is carried
Recommendation section rendered
Output does not claim ESOMAR-format conformance (compliant phrasing)
Output does not claim ESOMAR-format conformance (conformant phrasing)
No raw urn:mif: identifiers leak
No internal MIF finding IDs leak

expectations: The management summary explicitly states that ESOMAR is an ethics code, not a report format, and does not market the output as ESOMAR-format conformant.

The summary is the leadership/management-summary section only (BLUF, Key Findings, Recommendation, Risks & Caveats), sized for embedding into a fuller study, not the full multi-section market-research report.
No PTES sub-elements (Posture / Risk Profile / Roadmap) appear, because ESOMAR mode — not PTES mode — was requested.
